188	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
189	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
190	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
191	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
192	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
193	# Shut the pool down at interpreter exit (#F-043). wait=False avoids blocking exit on in-flight
194	# backend calls (already bounded by _with_timeout); without this, module globals can be torn down
195	# while a worker is mid-call, leaving behavior undefined. Mirrors checkpoint.py's atexit cleanup.
196	atexit.register(_pool.shutdown, wait=False)
197	_inflight = 0
198	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
199	
200	
201	def _release(future: Any) -> None:
202	    # Idempotent per future (#F-032 follow-up): decrement _inflight AT MOST ONCE for a given
203	    # future. The done-callback path and the interrupt-window fallback in _submit_bounded can BOTH
204	    # reach _release for the same future when add_done_callback appended the callback and then was
205	    # interrupted before returning — without this guard that double-decrements and over-releases a
206	    # slot (the inverse of the leak being fixed). A per-future flag set under the lock makes the
207	    # "_release runs at most once" claim true rather than aspirational.
208	    global _inflight
209	    with _inflight_lock:
210	        if getattr(future, "_assay_released", False):
211	            return
212	        try:
213	            future._assay_released = True
214	        except (AttributeError, TypeError):  # pragma: no cover - Future allows attrs; defensive
215	            pass
216	        _inflight -= 1
217	
218	
219	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
220	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
221	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
222	    global _inflight
223	    with _inflight_lock:
224	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
225	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
226	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
227	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
228	            raise RateLimitError(
229	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
230	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
231	            )
232	        _inflight += 1
233	    try:
234	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
235	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
236	        with _inflight_lock:
237	            _inflight -= 1
238	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
239	    except BaseException:
240	        with _inflight_lock:
241	            _inflight -= 1
242	        raise
243	    # Register the slot-release callback under its own guard (#F-032): if a BaseException
244	    # (KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit) fires while attaching the callback, release the slot
245	    # explicitly so a permanent leak can't accumulate and brick the pool with spurious
246	    # RateLimitErrors. If the interrupt landed AFTER add_done_callback already appended the
247	    # callback (so it will still fire on completion), the explicit _release here and the later
248	    # callback both target this future — _release is per-future idempotent, so the slot is
249	    # released exactly once either way.
250	    try:
251	        future.add_done_callback(_release)
252	    except BaseException:
253	        _release(future)
254	        raise
255	    return future
256	
257	
258	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
259	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
260	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
261	    try:
262	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
263	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
264	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
265	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
266	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
267	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
268	
269	
270	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
271	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
272	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
273	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
274	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
275	
276	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
277	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
278	    """
279	    start = None
280	    opener = closer = ""
281	    depth = 0
282	    in_string = False
283	    escape = False
284	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
285	        if start is None:
286	            if ch in "{[":
287	                start, opener = i, ch
288	                closer = "}" if ch == "{" else "]"
289	                depth = 1
290	            continue
291	        if in_string:
292	            if escape:
293	                escape = False
294	            elif ch == "\\":
295	                escape = True
296	            elif ch == '"':
297	                in_string = False
298	            continue
299	        if ch == '"':
300	            in_string = True
301	        elif ch == opener:
302	            depth += 1
303	        elif ch == closer:
304	            depth -= 1
305	            if depth == 0:
306	                return text[start : i + 1]
307	    return None
308	
309	
310	def extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
311	    """Parse JSON from a model reply (handles code fences and surrounding prose)."""
312	    span = _first_balanced_json(text)
313	    if span is None:
314	        raise ReasoningError("no JSON object/array found in reply")
315	    try:
316	        return json.loads(span)
317	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
318	        raise ReasoningError(f"reply contained malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
319	
320	
321	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	# Tier backends (lazy imports — module loads without them)
323	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
324	def _bulk_complete(
325	    prompt: str,
326	    system: str | None,
327	    temperature: float,
328	    model: str,
329	    json_mode: bool = False,
330	    *,
331	    seed: int | None = None,
332	    json_schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
333	) -> str:
334	    """Tier: BULK — local model runtime (loopback-enforced).
335	
336	    ``num_predict`` is always set (bounded generation). For JSON, a caller-supplied
337	    ``json_schema`` uses Ollama's schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path); otherwise
338	    ``json_mode`` falls back to loose ``format="json"``. ``seed`` lets a retry force a different
339	    deterministic decode without sharpening temperature.
340	    """
341	    require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
342	    try:
343	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
344	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
345	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
346	            "bulk tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (langchain-ollama) — not installed"
347	        ) from exc
348	
349	    # BULK's INNER bound (#G-003): the sync-tier counterpart to HIGH_STAKES's anyio.fail_after.
350	    # A ThreadPoolExecutor worker blocked in client.invoke() cannot be interrupted by the outer
351	    # _with_timeout, so the HTTP client timeout is what actually frees the slot on a hung backend.
352	    # A bare float sets EVERY httpx phase (connect/read/write/pool) to BULK_TIMEOUT, so a slow
353	    # connect THEN a slow read could total ~2*BULK_TIMEOUT — past the outer BULK_TIMEOUT+10 bound,
354	    # leaking the slot. Use an explicit Timeout so the TOTAL stays ~BULK_TIMEOUT (+ a small connect)
355	    # and aligns with the outer headroom. Fall back to the float if httpx is absent.
356	    try:
357	        import httpx
358	
359	        _bulk_timeout: Any = httpx.Timeout(
360	            BULK_TIMEOUT, connect=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT), pool=min(10.0, BULK_TIMEOUT)
361	        )
362	    except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - httpx ships with the reasoning extra
363	        _bulk_timeout = BULK_TIMEOUT
364	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = dict(
365	        model=model,
366	        base_url=BULK_BASE_URL,
367	        temperature=temperature,
368	        num_predict=BULK_NUM_PREDICT,  # bound generation length (H1)
369	        client_kwargs={"timeout": _bulk_timeout},
370	    )
371	    if seed is not None:
372	        kwargs["seed"] = seed
373	    if json_schema is not None:
374	        kwargs["format"] = json_schema  # Ollama schema-constrained decoding (reliable JSON)
375	    elif json_mode:
376	        kwargs["format"] = "json"  # loose native JSON decoding (syntactic only)
377	    client = ChatOllama(**kwargs)
378	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
379	    if system:
380	        messages.append(("system", system))
381	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
382	    try:
383	        reply = client.invoke(messages)
384	    except Exception as exc:
385	        # Prefer the typed HTTP status (ollama.ResponseError / httpx carry one): 404 = the model
386	        # isn't pulled → permanent. Fall back to substring matching only if no status is exposed
387	        # (robust to upstream message-wording changes — audit M4).
388	        status = getattr(exc, "status_code", None) or getattr(
389	            getattr(exc, "response", None), "status_code", None
390	        )
391	        msg = str(exc).lower()
392	        permanent = status == 404 or any(
393	            s in msg for s in ("not found", "no such model", "try pulling")
394	        )
395	        if permanent:
396	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"bulk model {model!r} not available: {exc}") from exc
397	        raise ReasoningError(f"bulk tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
398	    content = getattr(reply, "content", reply)
399	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
400	    text = text.strip()
401	    if not text:  # an empty reply is not a valid result — retry, don't return "" (issue #5/R5)
402	        raise ReasoningError("bulk tier returned an empty reply")
403	    return text
404	
405	
406	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
407	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
408	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
409	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
410	        return True
411	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
412	
413	
414	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
415	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
416	    try:
417	        import anyio
418	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
419	            AssistantMessage,
420	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
421	            ResultMessage,
422	            TextBlock,
423	            query,
424	        )
425	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
426	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
427	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
428	        ) from exc
429	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
430	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
431	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
432	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
433	        )
434	
435	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
436	        system_prompt=system,
437	        model=model,
438	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
439	        setting_sources=[],
440	        allowed_tools=[],
441	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
442	    )
443	
444	    parts: list[str] = []
445	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
446	
447	    async def _drain() -> None:
448	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
449	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
450	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
451	        #
452	        # Inner cancellation (#127): wrap the stream in anyio.fail_after so a hung subprocess is
453	        # actually CANCELLED (the cancellation propagates into query() and tears down the child),
454	        # freeing the bounded-pool worker. The outer _with_timeout only raises in the caller; it
455	        # cannot cancel this thread, so without this a hung HIGH_STAKES call would leak its slot
456	        # permanently. Fires before the outer wall-clock (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30).
457	        nonlocal result_error
458	        with anyio.fail_after(HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT):
459	            await _drain_stream()
460	
461	    async def _drain_stream() -> None:
462	        nonlocal result_error
463	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
464	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
465	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
466	                if rl == "rejected":
467	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
468	                continue
469	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
470	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
471	                continue
472	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
473	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
474	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
475	                        parts.append(block.text)
476	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
477	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
478	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
479	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
480	                ):
481	                    result_error = {
482	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
483	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
484	                    }
485	
486	    try:
487	        anyio.run(_drain)
488	    except Exception as exc:
489	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
490	
491	    if result_error is not None:
492	        status = result_error.get("status")
493	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
494	        if status in (429, 529):
495	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
496	        if status in (401, 403):
497	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
498	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
499	
500	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
501	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
502	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
503	    return text
504	
505	
506	def _attempt(request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
507	    """Route one request to its tier backend (no retry here)."""
508	    if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
509	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning is disabled (ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING)")
510	    p = request.params
511	    system = p.get("system")
512	    temperature = float(p.get("temperature", 0.0))
513	    model = p.get("model")
514	    json_mode = bool(p.get("_json_mode", False))
515	    seed = p.get("_seed")
516	    json_schema = p.get("json_schema")  # optional caller schema → constrained JSON decoding
517	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
518	        return cast(
519	            str,
520	            _with_timeout(
521	                lambda: _bulk_complete(
522	                    request.prompt,
523	                    system,
524	                    temperature,
525	                    model or BULK_MODEL,
526	                    json_mode,
527	                    seed=int(seed) if seed is not None else None,
528	                    json_schema=json_schema,
529	                ),
530	                BULK_TIMEOUT + 10,
531	                "bulk tier",
532	            ),
533	        )
534	    if request.tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
535	        # The high-stakes tier runs the Claude CLI subprocess (not the in-process Anthropic SDK,
536	        # so no auto-instrumentor covers it) — emit an explicit OpenInference LLM span so Phoenix
537	        # records the model/provider for this otherwise-untraced tier (#2).
538	        llm_attrs = {
539	            "llm.provider": "anthropic",
540	            "llm.system": "anthropic",
541	            "llm.model_name": str(model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL or "subscription-default"),
542	        }
543	        with _span("reasoning.high_stakes", llm_attrs, kind="LLM"):
544	            return cast(
545	                str,
546	                _with_timeout(
547	                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(
548	                        request.prompt, system, model or HIGH_STAKES_MODEL
549	                    ),
550	                    HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT + 30,
551	                    "high-stakes tier",
552	                ),
553	            )
554	    raise PermanentReasoningError(f"unknown tier: {request.tier!r}")
555	
556	
557	def _sleep_bounded(secs: float, deadline: float | None) -> None:
558	    """Sleep ``secs``, but never past ``deadline`` — so backoff can't blow an overall budget.
559	
560	    If the deadline has already passed, raise instead of sleeping the full interval, so a long
561	    rate-limit backoff cannot keep a caller blocked well beyond its deadline (#102).
562	    """
563	    if deadline is not None:
564	        remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
565	        if remaining <= 0:
566	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded during backoff")
567	        secs = min(secs, remaining)
568	    time.sleep(secs)
569	
570	
571	def _run_with_retries(request: ReasoningRequest, *, deadline: float | None = None) -> str:
572	    """Execute with independent transient and rate-limit retry budgets.
573	
574	    ``deadline`` (monotonic seconds) bounds both the cumulative backoff AND entry into each
575	    retry: once the deadline has passed, no further ``_attempt`` is started (#102, #F-022). It
576	    does not interrupt an attempt already in flight — that is bounded by the per-call timeout —
577	    so total wall-clock is at most ``deadline`` plus one in-flight attempt's timeout, not
578	    ``deadline`` plus the full retry budget of per-call timeouts.
579	    """
580	    transient = 0
581	    rate = 0
582	    attempted = False
583	    while True:
584	        # Gate retry ENTRY on the deadline (#F-022): the first attempt always runs, but a retry is
585	        # not started once the deadline is past — otherwise the per-attempt timeouts could stack
586	        # well beyond the overall budget even though the backoff sleeps were bounded.
587	        if attempted and deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
588	            raise ReasoningError("reasoning deadline exceeded before retry")
589	        attempted = True
590	        try:
591	            return _attempt(request)
592	        except PermanentReasoningError:
593	            raise
594	        except RateLimitError:
595	            if rate >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
596	                raise
597	            _sleep_bounded(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate + 1), deadline)
598	            rate += 1
599	        except ReasoningError:
600	            if transient >= MAX_RETRIES:
601	                raise
602	            _sleep_bounded(BACKOFF_BASE**transient, deadline)
603	            transient += 1
604	
605	
606	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
607	# Public seam
608	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
609	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
610	class ReasoningSeam(Protocol):
611	    """Route a reasoning request to the appropriate tier with timeouts, retries, tracing."""
612	
613	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str: ...
614	
615	
616	@contextmanager
617	def _span(name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any], *, kind: str = "AGENT") -> Iterator[None]:
618	    """Emit an OpenTelemetry span if a provider is registered; a cheap no-op otherwise.
619	
620	    Stamps the OpenInference ``openinference.span.kind`` (default ``AGENT`` — a reasoning call)
621	    so Phoenix classifies the span instead of rendering it UNKNOWN. OpenTelemetry is imported
622	    lazily, so the seam traces when the (self-hosted, on-box) observability stack is wired and is
623	    a silent no-op in tests / offline (ADR-0003).
624	    """
625	    try:
626	        from opentelemetry import trace
627	    except ImportError:
628	        yield
629	        return
630	    tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine.reasoning")
631	    # start_as_current_span auto-records exceptions + sets ERROR status by default (#F-005 was a
632	    # false positive — see assay_engine.observability.tracing for the verification note).
633	    with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as span:
634	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", kind)
635	        for k, v in attributes.items():
636	            span.set_attribute(k, v)
637	        yield
638	
639	
640	class TieredReasoningSeam:
641	    """Concrete tiered seam. ``run`` returns text; ``run_json`` parses a JSON reply.
642	
643	    Backends and tracing are resolved lazily, so an instance constructs with no external
644	    dependencies; calls fail loud (``PermanentReasoningError``) if a tier's backend or its
645	    subscription auth is absent. When the observability stack is present, each call emits an
646	    OpenTelemetry span; otherwise tracing is a no-op.
647	    """
648	
649	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
650	        attrs = {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose}
651	        with _span("reasoning.run", attrs):
652	            return _run_with_retries(request)
653	
654	    def run_json(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> Any:
655	        """Generate and parse JSON, re-rolling on parse failure.
656	
657	        If the caller supplies ``params["json_schema"]``, the BULK tier uses Ollama's
658	        schema-constrained decoding (the reliable path). Otherwise it falls back to loose JSON
659	        mode + prompt instruction. BULK decoding can be deterministic (temp 0), so re-issuing the
660	        identical prompt would yield a byte-identical bad reply; each retry **varies the seed**
661	        (forcing a different deterministic decode) and *monotonically* raises temperature toward
662	        1.0 — never lowering it, so re-rolls never sharpen back toward the same output. ``params``
663	        is frozen, so each attempt builds a fresh request (issue #R6).
664	        """
665	        base_temp = float(request.params.get("temperature", 0.0))
666	        system = request.params.get("system")
667	        json_system = (
668	            f"{system}\n\nRespond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
669	            if system
670	            else "Respond with ONLY a single JSON value, no prose."
671	        )
672	        last: ReasoningError | None = None
673	        deadline = time.monotonic() + RUN_JSON_DEADLINE  # overall bound across all re-rolls (#102)
674	        with _span(
675	            "reasoning.run_json",
676	            {"assay.tier": request.tier.value, "assay.purpose": request.purpose},
677	        ):
678	            for attempt in range(JSON_REROLLS + 1):
679	                if attempt > 0 and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
680	                    raise last or ReasoningError("run_json exceeded its overall deadline")
681	                temp = min(1.0, base_temp + 0.2 * attempt)  # monotonic, never decreases (M3)
682	                params = dict(request.params)
683	                params.update(temperature=temp, system=json_system, _json_mode=True, _seed=attempt)
684	                attempt_req = ReasoningRequest(
685	                    prompt=request.prompt,
686	                    tier=request.tier,
687	                    purpose=request.purpose,
688	                    params=params,
689	                )
690	                # A transient/rate/permanent error is already handled (and bounded by the shared
691	                # deadline) inside _run_with_retries — let it PROPAGATE rather than re-rolling, so
692	                # the budgets do NOT multiply (#102). We re-roll ONLY on a JSON *parse* failure.
693	                text = _run_with_retries(attempt_req, deadline=deadline)
694	                try:
695	                    return extract_json(text)
696	                except ReasoningError as exc:  # parse failure → re-roll with a new seed
697	                    last = exc
698	            raise last if last is not None else ReasoningError("run_json failed")
699	
700	    @staticmethod
701	    def is_available(tier: StakesTier) -> bool:
702	        """Cheap liveness check (spends no LLM turn). Never raises — returns False on error."""
703	        if _truthy("ASSAY_DISABLE_REASONING"):
704	            return False
705	        try:
706	            if tier is StakesTier.BULK:
707	                require_loopback_url(BULK_BASE_URL, what="bulk-tier model base URL")
708	                import urllib.request
709	
710	                with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BULK_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=2) as resp:
711	                    return bool(resp.status == 200)
712	            if tier is StakesTier.HIGH_STAKES:
713	                import shutil
714	
715	                return shutil.which("claude") is not None and _high_stakes_auth_present()
716	        except Exception:
717	            return False
718	        return False
719	
720	
721	class UnconfiguredReasoningSeam:
722	    """Explicit fail-loud seam for contexts that must not perform reasoning."""
723	
724	    def run(self, request: ReasoningRequest) -> str:
725	        raise NotImplementedError(
726	            "this context uses an UnconfiguredReasoningSeam — supply a TieredReasoningSeam "
727	            "(or a test double) to perform reasoning"
728	        )

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	
61	    Provenance note (#111): a gate node records its decision into LangGraph ``gate_decisions``
62	    state, which is **not** the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail. To get GOVERNANCE §3
63	    integrity on the durable path, pass ``make_gate_node`` a ``recorder`` that writes each
64	    decision into a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`; without it, the graph
65	    path's gate decisions live only as graph state.
66	    """
67	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
70	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
71	        )
72	    _require_langgraph()
73	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
74	
75	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
76	    build(builder)
77	    if checkpointer is not None:
78	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
79	    return builder.compile()
80	
81	
82	def _thread_config(run_id: str, recursion_limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    # Resume/run are keyed ONLY by thread_id — the checkpointer loads the latest (interrupted)
84	    # state itself. We deliberately do NOT thread a checkpoint_id: passing one is the
85	    # time-travel/replay API, and Command(resume=...) WITH a checkpoint_id replays from that
86	    # historical checkpoint instead of resuming the interrupt (version-dependent; LangGraph
87	    # issue #7361) — which would re-execute already-completed gates and break governance order.
88	    return {
89	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
90	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def run(
95	    graph: Any,
96	    state: Any,
97	    *,
98	    run_id: str,
99	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
100	    trace: bool = True,
101	) -> Any:
102	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
103	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
104	    resume it with :func:`resume`.
105	
106	    Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
107	    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
108	    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
109	    """
110	    if trace:
111	        bootstrap_tracing()
112	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
113	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
114	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")
115	
116	
117	def resume(
118	    graph: Any,
119	    *,
120	    run_id: str,
121	    gate_id: str,
122	    decision: str,
123	    rationale: str,
124	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
125	    trace: bool = True,
126	) -> Any:
127	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
128	
129	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
130	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
131	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
132	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``); resume always continues the
133	    latest interrupted state for the thread (no checkpoint_id — see :func:`_thread_config`).
134	    """
135	    _require_langgraph()
136	    from langgraph.types import Command
137	
138	    if trace:
139	        bootstrap_tracing()
140	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
141	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
142	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
143	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config, durability="sync")
144

1	"""Pre-registration integrity (METHODOLOGY.md §3, GOVERNANCE.md §2).
2	
3	GOVERNANCE §2 defines pre-registration as three steps: a hypothesis is (1) made specific and
4	typed, (2) **content-hashed and committed**, and (3) **timestamped against a trusted authority**
5	— *before* any confirmatory test. Steps 2–3 were a presence sentinel until now: ``Hypothesis.locked``
6	asserted only that ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` were non-``None``, so a hand-written
7	``timestamp_proof="rfc3161:demo"`` "locked" a hypothesis and nothing stopped a study from
8	locking *after* it had seen the baseline, or from swapping the hypothesis content after locking
9	(audit pass 1, issue #6). Both firewalls (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) and all of
10	:mod:`confirm` rest on the lock, so a sentinel lock made the three verdicts decorative.
11	
12	This module makes the lock mean what the method says, by construction:
13	
14	- **Content binding** — :func:`canonical_hypothesis_digest` is a type-faithful SHA-256 over a
15	  hypothesis's *decision-bearing* fields (statement, kind, origin, test, decision rule,
16	  predicted direction, source claim, params). A valid proof must attest to *this* digest, so
17	  the content the verdict is about is provably the content that was locked: change the claim,
18	  the test, or the decision rule after locking and verification fails.
19	- **Verifiable timestamp** — a pluggable :class:`TimestampAuthority` returns a
20	  :class:`VerifiedTimestamp` only if the proof genuinely binds the digest. The engine ships one
21	  real, data-sovereign authority (:class:`LocalHmacAuthority`, on-box HMAC) and the *Protocol*
22	  so a study can plug an RFC-3161 TSA. It ships **no** silent-accept authority.
23	- **Lock before confirm** — :func:`require_preregistered` rejects a lock whose attested time is
24	  not strictly before the confirmation moment, so a hypothesis cannot be "pre"-registered after
25	  the result is known.
26	
27	Honest scope (the lesson carried from ADR-0008): :class:`LocalHmacAuthority` proves content
28	integrity, time binding, and unforgeability *relative to a secret that lives on this box*.
29	Whoever holds that secret could backdate a proof — it is local tamper-evidence, **not**
30	third-party non-repudiation. A study that needs non-repudiation wires an RFC-3161 authority
31	(which sends only the digest, never the data, so it stays compatible with data sovereignty —
32	ADR-0003 — at the cost of an external dependency the study opts into). And content binding
33	covers the *structured fields*: free text inside ``statement``/``params`` is hashed (so it
34	cannot change after locking) but the engine cannot judge whether that text is *meaningful* —
35	that remains a review obligation (GOVERNANCE §2 "timestamped adaptive design").
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import datetime as _dt
41	import hmac
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, replace
43	from typing import Protocol
44	
45	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json, hash_text
46	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import FirewallViolation
47	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis
48	
49	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
50	_PROOF_PREFIX = "local-hmac:v1:"
51	_PROOF_FIELD_SEP = "|"  # ISO-8601 and lowercase hex never contain "|"
52	
53	
54	class PreRegistrationError(FirewallViolation):
55	    """A hypothesis failed pre-registration verification (METHODOLOGY §3, GOVERNANCE §2).
56	
57	    A subclass of :class:`FirewallViolation` so existing ``except FirewallViolation`` handlers
58	    in the runners and studies treat a bad lock as the firewall breach it is.
59	    """
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
63	class VerifiedTimestamp:
64	    """The authority's attestation that ``digest`` existed at ``instant``."""
65	
66	    instant: _dt.datetime  # timezone-aware, normalized to UTC
67	    authority: str
68	    digest: str
69	
70	
71	class TimestampAuthority(Protocol):
72	    """Engine contract: verify that ``proof`` binds ``digest``, returning the attested time.
73	
74	    Implementations MUST raise :class:`PreRegistrationError` (not return a sentinel) when the
75	    proof is malformed, does not bind ``digest``, or is otherwise invalid — the engine treats a
76	    non-raising return as a genuine attestation.
77	    """
78	
79	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp: ...
80	
81	
82	class StampingAuthority(TimestampAuthority, Protocol):
83	    """A study-side authority that can also *issue* proofs (used by :func:`lock_hypothesis`)."""
84	
85	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str: ...
86	
87	
88	def _require_aware_utc(instant: _dt.datetime, what: str) -> _dt.datetime:
89	    if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
90	        raise PreRegistrationError(
91	            f"{what} must be timezone-aware (a naive datetime has no defined instant)"
92	        )
93	    return instant.astimezone(_UTC)
94	
95	
96	def canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> str:
97	    """A type-faithful SHA-256 over a hypothesis's decision-bearing content **and its id**.
98	
99	    Includes ``hypothesis_id``: although it is a label, it is fixed at lock time and is the key
100	    the runners attribute verdicts by (``verdict.hypothesis_id``), so leaving it unbound would
101	    let a post-lock id swap file a verdict under an id the proof never attested (adversarial
102	    review, #80). Excludes only the lock fields themselves (``locked_at``/``timestamp_proof`` —
103	    they attest *to* this digest, so binding them would be circular).
104	
105	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the content cannot be canonicalized reproducibly
106	    (a non-finite float or an unhashable leaf in ``params``) — the pre-registration entrypoints
107	    must surface a typed firewall error, not a bare ``ValueError``/``TypeError`` (#82).
108	    """
109	    payload = {
110	        "hypothesis_id": hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
111	        "statement": hypothesis.statement,
112	        "kind": hypothesis.kind.value,
113	        "origin": hypothesis.origin.value,
114	        "test_name": hypothesis.test_name,
115	        "decision_rule": hypothesis.decision_rule,
116	        "source_claim_id": hypothesis.source_claim_id,
117	        "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction,
118	        "params": dict(hypothesis.params),
119	    }
120	    try:
121	        return hash_text(canonical_json(payload))
122	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
123	        raise PreRegistrationError(
124	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} cannot be canonicalized for a content "
125	            f"digest ({exc}); pre-registration requires reproducibly hashable content "
126	            "(no non-finite floats or unhashable leaves in params)"
127	        ) from None
128	
129	
130	class LocalHmacAuthority:
131	    """A real, on-box timestamp authority binding a digest and an instant under a secret key.
132	
133	    ``stamp`` issues ``local-hmac:v1:<instant-iso>|<hmac-hex>`` where the MAC is
134	    ``HMAC-SHA256(secret, digest || instant)``. ``verify`` recomputes the MAC over the digest
135	    the engine derived from the *current* hypothesis and the instant carried in the proof, and
136	    constant-time compares. The proof therefore verifies iff the content is unchanged, the
137	    instant is unchanged, and the same secret is used — without the secret it cannot be forged
138	    for arbitrary content or time.
139	
140	    Honest scope: this is local tamper-evidence, not third-party non-repudiation — the holder of
141	    ``secret`` could backdate (see module docstring). Use an RFC-3161 authority when you need a
142	    third party to vouch for the time.
143	    """
144	
145	    authority_id = "local-hmac"
146	
147	    def __init__(self, secret: bytes) -> None:
148	        if not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16:
149	            raise ValueError("LocalHmacAuthority secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
150	        self._secret = bytes(secret)
151	
152	    def _mac(self, digest: str, instant_iso: str) -> str:
153	        msg = canonical_json(["local-hmac:v1", digest, instant_iso]).encode("utf-8")
154	        return hmac.new(self._secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
155	
156	    def stamp(self, digest: str, *, instant: _dt.datetime | None = None) -> str:
157	        when = instant if instant is not None else _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
158	        when = _require_aware_utc(when, "stamp instant")
159	        instant_iso = when.isoformat()
160	        return f"{_PROOF_PREFIX}{instant_iso}{_PROOF_FIELD_SEP}{self._mac(digest, instant_iso)}"
161	
162	    def verify(self, digest: str, proof: str) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
163	        if not proof.startswith(_PROOF_PREFIX):
164	            raise PreRegistrationError(
165	                f"proof is not a {self.authority_id} v1 token (wrong prefix)"
166	            )
167	        body = proof[len(_PROOF_PREFIX) :]
168	        instant_iso, sep, mac_hex = body.partition(_PROOF_FIELD_SEP)
169	        if not sep or not instant_iso or not mac_hex:
170	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (expected <instant>|<mac>)")
171	        # The MAC field must be exactly the hex of a SHA-256 digest. Validating its shape here
172	        # (a) rejects garbage early and (b) keeps non-ASCII/non-hex out of hmac.compare_digest,
173	        # which raises TypeError on non-ASCII str rather than returning False (#81).
174	        if len(mac_hex) != 64 or any(c not in "0123456789abcdef" for c in mac_hex):
175	            raise PreRegistrationError("malformed local-hmac proof (mac is not 64 lowercase hex)")
176	        try:
177	            parsed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(instant_iso)
178	        except ValueError:
179	            raise PreRegistrationError(f"proof instant is not ISO-8601: {instant_iso!r}") from None
180	        instant = _require_aware_utc(parsed, "proof instant")
181	        expected = self._mac(digest, instant_iso)
182	        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, mac_hex):
183	            # Either the content (digest) changed since locking, the instant was tampered, or the
184	            # proof was issued under a different secret. All are pre-registration failures.
185	            raise PreRegistrationError(
186	                "proof does not bind this hypothesis's content at the stated time "
187	                "(content changed after locking, or proof was forged/issued by another authority)"
188	            )
189	        return VerifiedTimestamp(instant=instant, authority=self.authority_id, digest=digest)
190	
191	
192	def lock_hypothesis(
193	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
194	    *,
195	    authority: StampingAuthority,
196	    instant: _dt.datetime | None = None,
197	) -> Hypothesis:
198	    """Return a locked copy of ``hypothesis`` whose proof binds its current content.
199	
200	    The engine's blessed way to pre-register: it computes the content digest, has ``authority``
201	    stamp it, and records the attested instant in ``locked_at`` so the displayed lock time and
202	    the cryptographic proof always agree. Re-locking an already-locked hypothesis is refused.
203	
204	    Scope of the seal (same honest boundary as ADR-0008): this refuses re-locking *this object*.
205	    It cannot stop a study that willfully reconstructs a hypothesis — e.g. ``dataclasses.replace``
206	    to clear the lock fields, then re-lock different content — any more than the runners can stop
207	    a builder that reflects into their frame; both need process isolation, which is out of scope.
208	    What the engine *does* guarantee is that whatever is ultimately confirmed has a proof binding
209	    its content at an attested time before confirmation. So a re-locked copy is simply a new
210	    pre-registration with a new, later timestamp — visible as such, not a silent override.
211	    """
212	    if hypothesis.locked:
213	        raise PreRegistrationError(
214	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is already locked; re-locking is forbidden"
215	        )
216	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
217	    proof = authority.stamp(digest, instant=instant)
218	    verified = authority.verify(digest, proof)  # fail loud here if the authority is misbehaving
219	    return replace(hypothesis, locked_at=verified.instant.isoformat(), timestamp_proof=proof)
220	
221	
222	def verify_preregistration(
223	    hypothesis: Hypothesis, *, authority: TimestampAuthority
224	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
225	    """Verify a hypothesis is genuinely pre-registered: present, content-bound, time-attested.
226	
227	    Raises :class:`PreRegistrationError` if the hypothesis is not locked, the proof does not
228	    bind the *current* content digest, the attested instant is not a timezone-aware time, or the
229	    self-reported ``locked_at`` disagrees with the authority's attested instant.
230	    """
231	    if not hypothesis.locked:
232	        raise PreRegistrationError(
233	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
234	            "before pre-registration (METHODOLOGY §3 / GOVERNANCE §2)"
235	        )
236	    assert hypothesis.timestamp_proof is not None  # .locked guarantees this; for the type checker
237	    digest = canonical_hypothesis_digest(hypothesis)
238	    verified = authority.verify(digest, hypothesis.timestamp_proof)
239	    if verified.digest != digest:
240	        raise PreRegistrationError(
241	            f"authority attested digest {verified.digest!r} but the hypothesis content hashes to "
242	            f"{digest!r} — the proof does not cover this hypothesis"
243	        )
244	    # The displayed lock time must match the attested one, so a study cannot show a time the
245	    # authority did not vouch for.
246	    try:
247	        claimed = _dt.datetime.fromisoformat(hypothesis.locked_at or "")
248	    except ValueError:
249	        raise PreRegistrationError(
250	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} is not an ISO-8601 timestamp"
251	        ) from None
252	    if _require_aware_utc(claimed, "locked_at") != verified.instant:
253	        raise PreRegistrationError(
254	            f"locked_at {hypothesis.locked_at!r} disagrees with the authority-attested instant "
255	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}"
256	        )
257	    return verified
258	
259	
260	def require_preregistered(
261	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
262	    *,
263	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
264	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
265	) -> VerifiedTimestamp:
266	    """Gate a confirmatory step: the hypothesis must be verifiably locked *before* ``not_after``.
267	
268	    ``not_after`` is the confirmation moment (a runner captures it at entry). A lock whose
269	    attested instant is not strictly earlier is rejected — you cannot pre-register a hypothesis
270	    after the result that would confirm it is already in hand.
271	    """
272	    not_after = _require_aware_utc(not_after, "not_after")
273	    verified = verify_preregistration(hypothesis, authority=authority)
274	    if not verified.instant < not_after:
275	        raise PreRegistrationError(
276	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} was locked at "
277	            f"{verified.instant.isoformat()!r}, not strictly before the confirmation moment "
278	            f"{not_after.isoformat()!r} — a lock must precede confirmation (GOVERNANCE §2)"
279	        )
280	    return verified
281

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
75	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
76	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
77	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
78	        try:
79	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
80	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
81	        except Exception:
82	            try:
83	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
84	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
85	                pass
86	            raise
87	        return run_id
88	
89	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
90	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
91	
92	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
93	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
94	
95	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
96	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
97	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
98	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
99

1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
42	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
43	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
44	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
45	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
46	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
47	            f"from {url!r}"
48	        )
49	    return url
50	
51	
52	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
53	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
54	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
55	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
56	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
57	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
58	        )
59	    return host
60	
61	
62	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
63	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
64	
65	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
66	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
67	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
68	
69	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
70	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
71	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
72	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
73	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
74	    """
75	    if "@" in netloc:
76	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
77	    hosts: list[str] = []
78	    for part in netloc.split(","):
79	        p = part.strip()
80	        if not p:
81	            continue
82	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
83	            end = p.find("]")
84	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
85	        else:
86	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
87	    return hosts
88	
89	
90	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
91	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
92	
93	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
94	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
95	    reports, since the two can disagree:
96	
97	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
98	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
99	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
100	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
101	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
102	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
103	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
104	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
105	
106	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
107	    """
108	    try:
109	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
110	        netloc = parsed.netloc
111	        query = parsed.query
112	    except ValueError:
113	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
114	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
115	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
116	        ) from None
117	
118	    if netloc:
119	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
120	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
121	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
122	        if "\\" in netloc:
123	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
124	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
125	            )
126	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
127	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
128	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
129	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
130	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
131	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
132	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
133	                )
134	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
135	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
136	                h = val.strip()
137	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
138	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
139	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
140	                        f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
141	                    )
142	        return uri
143	
144	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
145	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
146	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
147	                h = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
148	                if h and not is_loopback_host(h):
149	                    raise NonLocalEndpointError(
150	                        f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
151	                        f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
152	                    )
153	        return uri
154	
155	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
156	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
157	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
158	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
159	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
160	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
161	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
163	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
164	        )
165	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
166

1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333"))
19	
20	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
21	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class VectorStore(Protocol):
26	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
27	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
28	
29	
30	def vector_store_url() -> str:
31	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
32	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
33	
34	
35	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
36	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
37	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
38	    try:
39	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
40	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
41	        raise RuntimeError(
42	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
43	        ) from exc
44	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
45	
46	
47	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
48	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
49	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
50	
51	
52	class QdrantVectorStore:
53	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
54	
55	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
56	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
57	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
58	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
62	        self._collection = collection
63	        self._dim = dim
64	        # Track ownership: only close a client we created ourselves; an injected client is the
65	        # caller's to manage (#117).
66	        self._owns_client = client is None
67	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
68	
69	    def close(self) -> None:
70	        """Close the underlying client if this store created it (no-op for an injected one)."""
71	        if self._owns_client:
72	            close = getattr(self._client, "close", None)
73	            if callable(close):
74	                close()
75	
76	    def __enter__(self) -> "QdrantVectorStore":
77	        return self
78	
79	    def __exit__(self, *_exc: object) -> None:
80	        self.close()
81	
82	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
83	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
84	
85	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
86	            self._client.create_collection(
87	                self._collection,
88	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
89	            )
90	
91	    def upsert(
92	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
93	    ) -> None:
94	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
95	
96	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
97	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
98	        if batch_size < 1:
99	            raise ValueError("batch_size must be >= 1")
100	        # Build each batch of PointStructs lazily, so only one batch is materialized in memory at
101	        # a time (bounding BOTH payload AND memory) rather than boxing the whole corpus up front
102	        # (#118). Qdrant guidance: don't upload point-by-point, but bound the batch.
103	        for start in range(0, len(ids), batch_size):
104	            batch = [
105	                PointStruct(id=_point_id(ids[j]), vector=list(vectors[j]), payload={"ref": ids[j]})
106	                for j in range(start, min(start + batch_size, len(ids)))
107	            ]
108	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=batch)
109	
110	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
111	        res = self._client.query_points(
112	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
113	        )
114	        return [(p.payload["ref"], float(p.score)) for p in res.points]
115

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import warnings
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
37	
38	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
39	
40	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
41	
42	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
43	
44	
45	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
46	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
47	
48	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
49	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
50	    """
51	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
52	        raise GateError(
53	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
54	        )
55	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
56	    if target != gate.name:
57	        raise GateError(
58	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
59	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
60	        )
61	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
62	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
63	        raise GateError(
64	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
65	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
66	        )
67	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
68	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
69	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
70	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
71	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
72	
73	
74	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
75	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
76	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
77	
78	
79	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
80	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
81	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
82	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
83	            return True
84	    return False
85	
86	
87	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
88	
89	
90	def make_gate_node(
91	    gate: Gate,
92	    propose: GateProposal,
93	    *,
94	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
95	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
96	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
97	
98	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
99	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
100	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
101	
102	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
103	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
104	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
105	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
106	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
107	
108	    Omitting ``recorder`` is a GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap: gate decisions then live only in
109	    (non-tamper-evident) LangGraph state, never in the hash-chained trail, so a checkpoint edit
110	    flipping ``rejected``→``approved`` is undetectable by ``verify_records``. Because that
111	    insecure configuration is effortless to ship by accident, building a node without a recorder
112	    emits a warning rather than failing silently (pass 3, #F-007).
113	    """
114	    if recorder is None:
115	        warnings.warn(
116	            f"make_gate_node({gate.name!r}) built with no recorder: gate decisions will NOT be "
117	            "written to the hash-chained provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap) — pass a "
118	            "recorder that calls trail.record('gate', ...) for a tamper-evident audit trail",
119	            stacklevel=2,
120	        )
121	
122	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
124	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
125	        try:
126	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
127	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
128	            raise RuntimeError(
129	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
130	            ) from exc
131	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
132	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
133	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
134	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
135	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
136	        while True:
137	            resumed = interrupt(current)
138	            try:
139	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
140	            except GateError as exc:
141	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
142	                continue
143	            if recorder is not None:
144	                # Record to the trail BEFORE returning the state update (#G-020): the validated
145	                # decision must land in the hash-chained trail and the graph state atomically. If
146	                # the recorder fails, surface a clear GateError INSTEAD of returning the update —
147	                # so the decision is neither half-applied (in graph state but absent from the
148	                # tamper-evident trail) nor silently lost; the operator/runner sees an explicit
149	                # failure to durably record the approval rather than a quietly diverging trail.
150	                try:
151	                    for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
152	                        recorder(rec)
153	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize any recorder backend failure
154	                    raise GateError(
155	                        f"gate {gate.name!r}: decision validated but could NOT be recorded to the "
156	                        f"provenance trail ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}); not applying the state "
157	                        "update so graph state cannot diverge from the trail"
158	                    ) from exc
159	            return update
160	
161	    return node
162

content.text: I found a notable mismatch in gatenode.py: the comment at line 144-149 claims the decision lands in trail and graph state "atomically", but the recorder runs first, then `return update`. If the recorder succeeds but the graph state write later fails, they diverge — not actually atomic. But more concretely, the claimed atomicity is impossible across two stores. Let me note this as a candidate. Let me check the docs now and gates.py, plus look at how `already_decided` interacts with the terminal-idempotency claim.
